metadata.summary: First-login Ubuntu 24.04 VPS setup: 5 phases (access+users, system identity, packages, security hardening, services foundation). Always test SSH as new user before disabling root login.

content.title: Server Init Bootstrap
content.summary: Complete first-login setup for a fresh Ubuntu 24.04 VPS: 5-phase sequence (access+users → system identity → packages+tools → security hardening → services foundation). Critical sequence constraint: always test SSH login as the new user BEFORE disabling root login — one wrong sshd_config line will lock you out. Requires loginctl enable-linger for systemd user services to survive logout.

why.p: An unscripted first login takes 2+ hours and leaves gaps — forgotten fail2ban, wrong locale, root SSH still enabled. A documented bootstrap sequence is reproducible, auditable, and serves as the server runbook. Cloud-init can automate it on provisioning, eliminating the first manual SSH session entirely.

list.item: First login to any new VPS (Hetzner, DigitalOcean, Linode, Vultr)
Rebuilding a server after a breach or OS reinstall
Automating server provisioning with cloud-init user-data
Auditing an existing server against the bootstrap checklist
Managed platforms (Heroku, Railway, Render) — OS is abstracted, bootstrap doesn't apply
Kubernetes nodes — managed by the cluster control plane, not manual setup
Before verifying SSH key access as the new user — never disable root login first
Running the full bootstrap on a live production server — only during initial setup

section.name: phase-1-access-and-users
section.p: First 5 minutes. SSH as root, create non-root user, deploy SSH key, TEST login as new user before any other action.

rule.statement: Always test SSH as the new user in a separate terminal BEFORE disabling root login.
rule.rationale: A misconfigured sshd_config (wrong AllowUsers, wrong key) will lock you out permanently unless you have the server provider's console. Testing first guarantees a recovery path.

list.item: SSH as root → create non-root user with sudo
Deploy SSH public key to new user's authorized_keys
Open a new terminal, test: ssh newuser@server-ip
Verify sudo works: sudo whoami → should output "root"
Only now: disable root SSH login and password auth
Set hostname: hostnamectl set-hostname server-name
Update /etc/hosts: add 127.0.1.1 server-name
Set timezone: timedatectl set-timezone Europe/Lisbon
Set locale: locale-gen en_US.UTF-8 && update-locale LANG=en_US.UTF-8
Enable NTP: timedatectl set-ntp true
apt update && apt upgrade -y — apply all pending updates first
Install essentials: build-essential, curl, git, tmux, htop, jq, rsync, ncdu
Install Python dev: python3-dev, python3-venv, libpq-dev
Install mise: curl https://mise.run | sh
Install direnv: apt install -y direnv
Add hooks to .bashrc: mise before direnv
Configure UFW: deny incoming, allow SSH port, allow 80/443, enable
Install fail2ban, configure jail.local (3 retries, 1h ban)
Install unattended-upgrades, enable auto-reboot at 4 AM
Apply sshd_config hardening: no root, no password, MaxAuthTries 3
Test SSH config: sshd -t before reloading

rule.statement: Enable loginctl linger for the service user before creating any systemd user services.
rule.rationale: Without linger, user services (--user) only run during an active login session. They stop when you log out and do not start at boot. Linger makes them persistent.

list.item: loginctl enable-linger username — services survive logout and start at boot
mkdir -p ~/.config/systemd/user — create user service directory
Install Docker if needed (bind all ports to 127.0.0.1)
Configure journal size: SystemMaxUse=500M in journald.conf

example.code: # /etc/ssh/sshd_config — key changes Port 22022 PermitRootLogin no PasswordAuthentication no PubkeyAuthentication yes MaxAuthTries 3 ClientAliveInterval 300 ClientAliveCountMax 2 AllowUsers nero X11Forwarding no

rule.statement: Always run sshd -t to test config before systemctl reload sshd.
rule.rationale: A syntax error in sshd_config causes sshd to refuse to reload, but the running process continues to accept connections on the old config — you won't notice the error until the next restart.

example.code: # ALWAYS add SSH rule BEFORE enabling sudo ufw default deny incoming sudo ufw default allow outgoing sudo ufw limit 22022/tcp comment 'SSH' sudo ufw allow 80/tcp comment 'HTTP' sudo ufw allow 443/tcp comment 'HTTPS' sudo ufw logging low sudo ufw --force enable sudo ufw status verbose

# /etc/fail2ban/jail.local [DEFAULT] bantime = 3600 findtime = 600 maxretry = 3
[sshd] enabled = true port = 22022 filter = sshd logpath = /var/log/auth.log maxretry = 3

# Enable linger — user services run at boot, survive logout sudo loginctl enable-linger nero
# Verify loginctl show-user nero | grep Linger # Linger=yes

echo "=== Hostname ===" && hostname echo "=== Timezone ===" && timedatectl | grep "Time zone" echo "=== Locale ===" && locale | head -1 echo "=== UFW ===" && sudo ufw status | head -5 echo "=== fail2ban ===" && sudo fail2ban-client status sshd 2>/dev/null || echo "not running" echo "=== SSH ===" && grep -E "PermitRootLogin|PasswordAuthentication" /etc/ssh/sshd_config echo "=== Swap ===" && swapon --show echo "=== Memory ===" && free -h | head -2 echo "=== Disk ===" && df -h / | tail -1 echo "=== Linger ===" && loginctl show-user nero 2>/dev/null | grep Linger

section.name: hetzner-cloud-init
section.p: Automate the entire bootstrap on first boot. Paste into Hetzner Cloud: Create server → Cloud config (User data).

example.code:
#cloud-config users: - name: nero groups: sudo, docker shell: /bin/bash sudo: ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL ssh_authorized_keys: - ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3... your_key_here
package_update: true package_upgrade: true packages: - ufw - fail2ban - unattended-upgrades - curl - git - tmux - htop - jq - rsync
runcmd: - ufw default deny incoming - ufw default allow outgoing - ufw limit 22022/tcp - ufw allow 80/tcp - ufw allow 443/tcp - ufw --force enable - loginctl enable-linger nero - dpkg-reconfigure -plow unattended-upgrades

template.purpose: Full interactive bootstrap script: all 5 phases with verification steps
cloud-init user-data: user, SSH key, packages, UFW, unattended-upgrades
Production sshd_config: no root, no password, MaxAuthTries 3, AllowUsers
jail.local: 1h ban, 3 retries, sshd jail enabled
Post-bootstrap checklist: hostname, timezone, locale, UFW, fail2ban, SSH config, swap, linger

script.purpose: Dashboard: uptime, disk, memory, swap, running services, UFW rules, open ports
